docs.title: Database Migration
docs.text:
For production deployments, run migrations:

npx prisma migrate deploy

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Ensure all required environment variables are set:

  • DATABASE_URL - PostgreSQL connection string
  • NEXTAUTH_URL - Application URL
  • NEXTAUTH_SECRET - Secret for session encryption
  • SENTRY_DSN - Sentry error tracking (optional)
  • NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN - Client-side Sentry (optional)

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
SmartBudget uses PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM. Configure your database connection in .env:

DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@host:port/database?schema=public"

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Configure NextAuth.js providers in src/auth.ts:

  • Username/Password (default)
  • Google OAuth (coming soon)
  • Apple Sign-In (coming soon)

docs.title: AI Integration
docs.text:
To enable Claude AI merchant research:

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your-api-key"

docs.title: Sentry (Error Monitoring)
docs.text:
Optional but recommended for production:

SENTRY_DSN="your-sentry-dsn"
NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN="your-public-dsn"
SENTRY_ORG="your-org"
SENTRY_PROJECT="your-project"

See ERROR_MONITORING.md for setup details.

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
SmartBudget is optimized for performance:

  • Page Load: <2s (First Contentful Paint)
  • API Response: <200ms (p95)
  • Database Queries: <50ms (p95)
  • Transaction Import: 10,000 transactions in <5s
  • Dashboard Render: <1s with 10,000+ transactions

docs.title: Performance Features
docs.text:
• Server-side rendering (SSR) for fast initial loads
  • Optimistic UI updates
  • API route caching
  • Database query optimization with indexes
  • Code splitting and lazy loading
  • Image optimization

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
SmartBudget takes security seriously:

  • Encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
  • Authentication: NextAuth.js with secure sessions
  • Authorization: Row-level security for all data access
  • Input Validation: Zod schemas for all inputs
  • SQL Injection Protection: Prisma parameterized queries
  • XSS Protection: React's built-in escaping + CSP headers
  • CSRF Protection: CSRF tokens on all mutations

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
• Never commit .env files
  • Rotate secrets regularly
  • Keep dependencies updated
  • Review code for security issues
  • Use strong passwords and 2FA
  • Follow OWASP Top 10 guidelines

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Your financial data privacy is paramount:

  • No Data Selling: Your data is never sold or shared with third parties
  • Encryption: All sensitive data is encrypted
  • Bank Credentials: Never stored (file-based imports only)
  • GDPR Compliant: Full data portability and deletion rights
  • Anonymized Analytics: Optional and aggregated only

docs.title: Data Handling
docs.text:
• Local Processing: Transaction categorization happens on your server
  • Minimal Cloud AI: Claude AI research is opt-in and merchant-specific
  • Data Export: Download all your data anytime
  • Account Deletion: Permanently delete all data

docs.title: General
docs.text:
Q: Is SmartBudget free? A: Yes, SmartBudget is currently free during the beta period. Future pricing TBD.

Q: Do I need to connect my bank account? A: No! SmartBudget uses file-based imports (CSV, OFX). No bank credentials required.

Q: Which banks are supported? A: Any bank that exports CSV or OFX/QFX files. Optimized for Canadian banks (CIBC, TD, RBC, Scotiabank, BMO).

docs.title: Technical
docs.text:
Q: Is my data secure? A: Yes. All data is encrypted (AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit). We never store bank credentials.

Q: Can I self-host SmartBudget? A: Yes! SmartBudget is open-source. Follow the installation guide above.

Q: Does it work offline? A: The web app requires internet. Future mobile apps will have offline support.

Q: How accurate is the auto-categorization? A: 90%+ accuracy after initial training. Improves as you correct transactions.

docs.title: Sophie Voice Bridge
docs.text: ElevenLabs Custom LLM server that connects to Sophie AI via Clawdbot.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
WhatsApp Call → ElevenLabs (STT/TTS) → Haiku (fast) ─┬─→ Direct response
                                                      │
                                                      └─→ ask_sophie → Sophie/Opus
                                                                          (full capabilities)
  • Haiku handles fast voice responses (1-3 sentences)
  • ask_sophie tool escalates complex questions to Sophie (Opus with tools)
  • Sophie has access to calendar, email, web search, files, memory, etc.

docs.title: Quick Start (Docker)
docs.text:
# Clone
git clone https://github.com/aaron777collins/sophie-voice-bridge.git
cd sophie-voice-bridge

# Configure
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your Clawdbot gateway token

# Run
docker compose up -d

# Check health
curl http://localhost:8013/health

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Create .env file:

CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_URL=http://localhost:18789
CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_TOKEN=your-gateway-token

docs.title: API Endpoints
docs.text:
Both routes work (ElevenLabs uses /chat/completions):

POST /v1/chat/completions    # OpenAI standard
POST /chat/completions       # ElevenLabs format
GET  /health                 # Health check

docs.title: Step 1: Create an Agent
docs.text:
1. Go to ElevenLabs Agents Platform
  2. Click "Create Agent"
  3. Give it a name (e.g., "Sophie Voice")

docs.title: Step 2: Configure LLM (Custom)
docs.text:
1. In Agent settings, go to "LLM" section
  2. Select "Custom LLM"
  3. Configure:
  4. Server URL: https://voice.aaroncollins.info
  5. Model: haiku (or anything - it's ignored)
  6. Custom LLM extra body: ✅ Enable this
  7. Limit token usage: 5000 (recommended)

docs.title: Step 3: Configure Voice
docs.text:
1. Go to "Voice" section
  2. Choose a voice you like (the voice Sophie will speak with)
  3. Adjust stability/clarity as needed

docs.title: Step 4: Connect WhatsApp (via Twilio)
docs.text:
1. Go to "Phone Numbers" section
  2. Click "Import Phone Number"
  3. Enter your Twilio credentials:
  4. Phone Number: Your Twilio number with WhatsApp enabled
  5. Twilio SID: From Twilio console
  6. Twilio Token: From Twilio console
  7. Assign the agent to the phone number

docs.title: Step 5: Test
docs.text:
Outbound call (you call someone): 1. In Phone Numbers, click the outbound call button 2. Select your agent 3. Enter the phone number to call 4. Click "Send Test Call"

Inbound call (someone calls you): - Call your Twilio number on WhatsApp - The agent will answer automatically

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. You speak on WhatsApp call
  2. ElevenLabs STT transcribes your speech to text
  3. Text sent to /chat/completions endpoint
  4. Haiku evaluates:
  5. Simple question → responds directly (fast, 1-3 sentences)
  6. Complex question → uses ask_sophie tool
  7. If ask_sophie used:
  8. Sophie (Opus) processes with full tool access
  9. Can check calendar, email, search web, access files
  10. Haiku relays response conversationally
  11. ElevenLabs TTS converts response to speech
  12. You hear Sophie's voice response

docs.title: When ask_sophie is Used
docs.text:
Haiku automatically escalates to Sophie when you ask about: - Calendar, schedule, events, appointments - Emails, messages, notifications - Files, documents, code, projects - Research requiring web search or browsing - Complex technical or business questions - Anything requiring memory of past conversations

Simple greetings, math, and general knowledge are handled by Haiku directly.

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
# Local setup
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run locally
python bridge.py

docs.title: Test Commands
docs.text:
# Health check
curl https://voice.aaroncollins.info/health

# Simple question (Haiku direct)
curl -X POST https://voice.aaroncollins.info/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2?"}], "stream": false}'

# Complex question (triggers ask_sophie)
curl -X POST https://voice.aaroncollins.info/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What meetings do I have tomorrow?"}], "stream": false}'

docs.title: tmux-session-manager
docs.text:
A fuzzy terminal popup to manage and create tmux sessions using fzf.

Just a simple and fast tmux workflow helper. It opens popups using fzf where you can:

  • View and switch sessions quickly
  • Preview windows in the selected session
  • Delete a session with confirmation
  • Create or jump to a session from a directory

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• tmux 3.2 or higher (for display-popup)
  • fzf
  • Common UNIX tools (awk, bash, find, tree)

docs.title: Via TPM (Tmux Plugin Manager)
docs.text:
1. Add plugin to your ~/.tmux.conf:
set -g @plugin 'vimlinuz/tmux-sm'
  1. Press prefix + I to install.

docs.title: Manual Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository:
git clone https://github.com/vimlinuz/tmux-sm ~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-sm
  1. Add to your ~/.tmux.conf:
run-shell ~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-sm/main.tmux
  1. Reload tmux config:
tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf

docs.title: Default Key Bindings
docs.text:
• Press prefix + s to open the session switcher
  • Press prefix + j to open the session creator

docs.title: Custom Key Bindings
docs.text:
Add to your ~/.tmux.conf to change default keys:

set -g @session_switcher_key 'S'  # Switcher key (default: 's')
set -g @session_create_key 'J'    # Creator key (default: 'j')

Legacy option names are still supported:

set -g @session_manager_key 'S'
set -g @session_creator_key 'J'

docs.title: Session Switcher Controls
docs.text:
Inside the switcher popup:

  • Type to search - Fuzzy find sessions by name
  • Enter - Switch to selected session
  • Ctrl-O - Open session creator
  • Ctrl-D - Delete selected session (with y/n confirmation)
  • Esc - Close without switching

docs.title: Session Creator Behavior
docs.text:
Inside the creator popup:

  • Type to search - Fuzzy find directories under $HOME
  • Enter - Create/switch to session named from directory basename
  • If tmux is not running, it starts a new tmux session
  • If the session exists, it switches/attaches to it
  • Esc - Close without creating/switching

docs.title: Available Options
docs.text:
# Session switcher key binding (default: 's')
set -g @session_switcher_key 's'

# Session creator key binding (default: 'j')
set -g @session_create_key 'j'

# Popup width (default: '80%')
set -g @session_popup_width '80%'

# Popup height (default: '60%')
set -g @session_popup_height '60%'

# Session switcher header (default: dynamic header with key hints)
set -g @session_switcher_header ''

# Session creator header (default: dynamic header with key hints)
set -g @session_creator_header ''

docs.title: Popup Size (Current Default)
docs.text:
Default popup size:

  • Width: 80%
  • Height: 60%

To change popup size, set options in your ~/.tmux.conf:

set -g @session_popup_width '90%'
set -g @session_popup_height '70%'

docs.title: Custom Header Text
docs.text:
You can override popup header text for both views:

set -g @session_switcher_header '󰆍 My Sessions | Ctrl-O new | Ctrl-D delete'
set -g @session_creator_header '󱂬 Project Picker'

If these options are empty, the plugin uses dynamic default headers with key hints.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Popup does not appear?

  • Make sure you have tmux 3.2+: tmux -V
  • Check popup command support: tmux list-commands | grep popup

fzf not found?

  • Install fzf: brew install fzf or apt install fzf
  • Or follow the official fzf installation guide

tree not found in preview?

  • Install tree package for your OS
  • Or remove/adjust the preview command in scripts/sessionizer

Key binding conflicts?

If prefix + s or prefix + j conflicts with existing bindings, change them:

set -g @session_switcher_key 'your-switcher-key'
set -g @session_create_key 'your-creator-key'

docs.title: word-map-api
docs.text:
Takes a word array and returns the synonyms/antonyms related to each.

For example:

}
